([abbreviation](/wiki/Abbreviation "Abbreviation"): **ICCC**) was founded on 12 August 1948 at the [English Reformed Church, Amsterdam](/wiki/English_Reformed_Church,_Amsterdam "English Reformed Church, Amsterdam"), as a [fundamentalist Christian](/wiki/Christian_fundamentalism "Christian fundamentalism") group of constituent national churches with opposition to the more [liberal](/wiki/Liberal_Christianity "Liberal Christianity")-leaning [World Council of Churches](/wiki/World_Council_of_Churches "World Council of Churches").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Organization

International Council of Christian Churches

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Abbreviation | ICCC |
| Formation | 12 August 1948 (1948-08-12) |
| Founder | [Carl McIntire](/wiki/Carl_McIntire "Carl McIntire") |
| Founded at | [English Reformed Church, Amsterdam](/wiki/English_Reformed_Church,_Amsterdam "English Reformed Church, Amsterdam") |
| Website | [iccc-churches.org](http://iccc-churches.org) |

The **International Council of Christian Churches** ([abbreviation](/wiki/Abbreviation "Abbreviation"): **ICCC**) was founded on 12 August 1948 at the [English Reformed Church, Amsterdam](/wiki/English_Reformed_Church,_Amsterdam "English Reformed Church, Amsterdam"), as a [fundamentalist Christian](/wiki/Christian_fundamentalism "Christian fundamentalism") group of constituent national churches with opposition to the more [liberal](/wiki/Liberal_Christianity "Liberal Christianity")-leaning [World Council of Churches](/wiki/World_Council_of_Churches "World Council of Churches").

One of the main leaders of the movement was [Carl McIntire](/wiki/Carl_McIntire "Carl McIntire"), a [Presbyterian](/wiki/Presbyterian "Presbyterian") minister, who also founded a number of [Bible colleges](/wiki/Bible_colleges "Bible colleges") and the [American Council of Christian Churches](/wiki/American_Council_of_Christian_Churches "American Council of Christian Churches").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)
